text.title: Meta Audience Targeting (3-Tier) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the ads-meta-targeting methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every campaign MUST launch with Core + Custom + Lookalike layers defined (some may be empty for awareness/cold only, but spec lists all three).
rule.rationale: Single-tier campaigns either over-spend on cold or miss warm conversion.

rule.statement: Lookalike source MUST have ≥1000 people. Below that, match quality is statistically poor.
rule.rationale: Meta documents that <1000-source LAL underperforms vs interest targeting.

rule.statement: Core/Interest audiences MUST be 500K-2M for cold prospecting. <500K = throttled delivery; >2M = unfocused.
rule.rationale: Auction floor + bid efficiency works best in the 500K-2M range.

rule.statement: Exclusion audiences (purchasers, subscribers, recent converters) MUST be set BEFORE launching any acquisition campaign.
rule.rationale: Without exclusions, acquisition spend hits current customers, inflates CPA, and re-bills retention.

rule.statement: Advantage+ Audience is allowed ONLY when product appeal is broad AND daily budget ≥ $100. Otherwise manual targeting.
rule.rationale: Advantage+ over-explores; cheap on volume, expensive on quality for niche products.
